name: Sun Loan Company
description_short: Community-based personal installment lender with 30+ years in business, offering loans from $150 to $3,999 with same-day funding and bilingual service.
description_long:
Sun Loan Company has operated for over 30 years as a personal installment lender with branches across multiple states including Alabama, Illinois, Missouri, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Texas, and Nevada. The company positions itself as a community financial resource, with bilingual (English/Spanish) staff and a focus on serving customers who may have limited access to traditional bank lending. Their Las Vegas branch is located at 1600 N. Nellis Boulevard in a neighborhood setting rather than a financial district.

Sun Loan offers personal installment loans ranging from $150 to $3,999.15 in Nevada, with same-day funding available either through an in-branch disbursement or a bank deposit. Applications can be submitted online or in person, and the company explicitly states it evaluates borrowers on more than their credit score alone. Beyond loans, each branch also provides tax preparation services with a refund advance of up to $7,000 for early filers, along with ancillary services like copies and fax.

What distinguishes Sun Loan from online-only lenders is its branch-first model with local, named staff and walk-in access. Customer reviews on the site consistently highlight fast turnaround, friendly service, and staff who explain options clearly. The company also provides access to MoneySKILL personal finance courses and an Auto Club, positioning itself as more than a transactional lender. Spanish-language service is available at all branches, broadening its reach to underserved communities.

The primary limitation is transparency: Sun Loan does not publish APRs, interest rates, or fee schedules on its public website, making it impossible to compare costs before applying. Small-dollar installment lenders in this segment commonly carry high APRs. The loan ceiling of roughly $4,000 also limits its usefulness for larger financial needs. Borrowers should request the full APR and total repayment cost before signing any agreement.
address: 2713 SW 29th St Ste 5, Oklahoma City, OK 73119

company_info.headquarters: 2713 SW 29th St Ste 5, Oklahoma City, OK 73119

pros: Over 30 years in business — established community lender with long track record
Same-day funding available via branch pickup or bank deposit
Loans start as low as $150 — accessible for very small borrowing needs
Evaluates more than credit score — accessible to credit-challenged applicants
Bilingual staff — full service in English and Spanish
Tax preparation and refund advance (up to $7,000) available at same location
Online and in-person application options with no credit inquiry to check eligibility
cons: No APR or interest rate disclosure on the website — cost is opaque before applying
Loan maximum of ~$3,999 is too low for borrowers with larger financing needs
Saturday hours are limited (select Saturdays only, 8:30 AM–12:30 PM) and Sundays closed
Branch-dependent model means access varies by location — not nationwide
Small-dollar installment lenders in this segment typically carry high APRs not disclosed upfront
best_for: Credit-challenged borrowers who need a small loan under $4,000 and can't qualify at banks
Spanish-speaking consumers who need bilingual in-person financial services
Borrowers who prefer face-to-face service and same-day cash from a local branch
Individuals combining loan needs with tax filing or refund advance services
diagnosis: Sun Loan is best suited for credit-challenged borrowers who need a small installment loan under $4,000 quickly and prefer the reassurance of a staffed local branch over an online-only lender. The main caveat is full rate opacity — the website discloses no APR, fees, or example repayment schedules, so borrowers must inquire directly before committing to understand the true cost of borrowing.
